Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify what happens if the PCAP file is invalid or corrupted
Correction: If the PCAP file is invalid or corrupted, the system shall display an error message and prompt the user to load a valid PCAP file.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the maximum file size for PCAP files
Correction: The system shall support loading PCAP files up to 2GB in size.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify what happens if the user tries to load a PCAP file while another is being processed
Correction: If a PCAP file is being processed, the system shall queue the new file and notify the user once the current processing is complete.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the supported file formats for saving sessions
Correction: The system shall support saving sessions in JSON and XML formats.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to save a session without any changes
Correction: If no changes have been made, the system shall notify the user that there are no changes to save.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to save a configuration with an existing name
Correction: If a configuration with the same name exists, the system shall prompt the user to overwrite the existing configuration or choose a different name.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to run the preprocessor without loading a PCAP file
Correction: If no PCAP file is loaded, the system shall display an error message and prevent the preprocessor from running.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to run the preprocessor with an incomplete configuration
Correction: If the preprocessor configuration is incomplete, the system shall display an error message indicating the missing configuration parameters.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to compare runs without any completed runs
Correction: If no runs have been completed, the system shall disable the compare runs feature and notify the user.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to export graphs without any data
Correction: If no data is available, the system shall disable the export feature and notify the user.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to zoom in/out beyond the graph limits
Correction: The system shall restrict zooming to within the graph limits and notify the user if they attempt to zoom beyond these limits.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to save a graph as an image without any data
Correction: If no data is available, the system shall disable the save as image feature and notify the user.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to open a saved session from an incompatible version
Correction: If the saved session is from an incompatible version, the system shall notify the user and provide an option to convert the session to the current version format.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to load a configuration file from an incompatible version
Correction: If the configuration file is from an incompatible version, the system shall notify the user and provide an option to convert the configuration to the current version format.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to open multiple instances of the program
Correction: If multiple instances of the program are opened, the system shall notify the user and provide an option to close the existing instance or continue with the new instance.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to save a session while the preprocessor is running
Correction: If the preprocessor is running, the system shall disable the save session feature and notify the user.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to load a PCAP file while the preprocessor is running
Correction: If the preprocessor is running, the system shall disable the load PCAP feature and notify the user.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to change the preprocessor configuration while it is running
Correction: If the preprocessor is running, the system shall disable the configuration panel and notify the user.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to close the program while the preprocessor is running
Correction: If the preprocessor is running, the system shall prompt the user to confirm if they want to stop the preprocessor and close the program.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to open a saved session while another session is active
Correction: If another session is active, the system shall prompt the user to save the current session before opening the new one.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to save a session with an existing name
Correction: If a session with the same name exists, the system shall prompt the user to overwrite the existing session or choose a different name.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to export a report without any data
Correction: If no data is available, the system shall disable the export report feature and notify the user.

Label: Section 2.2.1
Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to open a saved session with missing or corrupted data
Correction: If the saved session has missing or corrupted data, the system shall notify the user and provide an option to load a different session.

Issue: does not specify the behavior when the user tries to load a configuration with missing or corrupted data
Correction: If the configuration file has missing or corrupted data, the system shall notify the user and provide an option to load a different configuration.
